WelcomePack

All you need to know before the project

BANSKO, BULGARIA • PIRIN MOUNTAINS • 16–25 AUGUST 2026
Surf Smart, Stay Safe: Online Protection for Youth
Erasmus+ KA152 Youth Exchange • forum JUGEND! e.V. • Form ID: KA152-YOU-6FC2B5EB
📍 How to get to Royal Bansko

Accommodation: Royal Bansko Family Apart Hotel
Website: royalbansko.bg
Address: ul. Kosherina, 2770 Bansko, Bulgaria

Royal Bansko is located in the town of Bansko, at the foot of the Pirin Mountains — surrounded by mountain scenery and within walking distance of local restaurants, shops and the town centre.

🚌 Getting to Bansko by bus

Most participants will arrive at Sofia Central Bus Station (Tsentralna Avtogara). From there, take a direct bus to Bansko — the journey takes about 2.5–3 hours.

  • At Sofia Central Bus Station, buy a ticket at the counter or online at obilet.com.
  • Board a direct bus to Bansko (several departures daily).
  • Arrive at Bansko Bus Station (Автогара Банско).
  • Take a taxi directly to Royal Bansko (about 5 minutes).
🧾 Keep your taxi receipt! The taxi fare from the bus station to the hotel is reimbursable — ask the driver for a receipt and keep it safe.

✈️ If arriving by plane (Sofia Airport)

  • From Sofia Airport, take the Metro to Serdika station (city centre).
  • Walk or take a taxi to Sofia Central Bus Station (about 10 min).
  • Follow the bus steps above to reach Bansko.
📱 Useful tip: Check bus schedules at obilet.com or use Google Maps for real-time transit directions.

🏨 Arrival & check-in

On arrival, go directly to the reception desk to complete your registration. The organisers will be there to welcome you and help with room assignments.

🏖️ Accommodation & Surroundings

🏊 Hotel spa & pool

Royal Bansko has a spa & wellness centre with an indoor swimming pool, sauna and outdoor jacuzzis, available for the group during free time. Bring flip-flops and a swimming suit so you can enjoy the facilities!

🧻 Good to know: Towels are provided by the hotel. Bathrobes for the spa can be rented for the whole stay for just €2. Tap water is safe to drink — it comes directly from the Pirin Mountain springs — so bring a reusable bottle & cup and refill freely!

🌍 Places to visit nearby

♨️ Pulse Therme (Banya)

The largest spa & thermal-water complex on the Balkans, in nearby Banya — pools, saunas and mineral springs. A perfect free-time trip.

WebsiteOpen in Google Maps

🌊 Lake Okoto (“The Eye”)

A breathtaking glacial “Eye” lake high in the mountains — one of Bulgaria’s most beautiful lakes and a memorable hiking day-trip.

Open in Google Maps

⛰️ Pirin National Park

UNESCO World Heritage site — mountain trails, alpine lakes and ancient pine forests, a short drive from Bansko.

Open in Google Maps

🏔️ Bansko Gondola Lift

Ride up to Banderishka Polyana (1,100–1,700 m) for panoramic views — beautiful even in summer.

Open in Google Maps

⛪ Holy Trinity Church

Orthodox church built in 1835 with stunning frescoes, in the heart of Old Bansko.

Open in Google Maps

🚶 Bansko Old Town

Cobblestone streets, stone houses, cosy taverns (mehanas) and the pedestrian zone.

Open in Google Maps
🎒 What to Bring

Bansko in August is warm and sunny during the day (22–28°C) but can get cool in the evenings (12–16°C) due to the mountain altitude (~925 m). Pack layers!

👕 Comfortable casual clothes
🧥 Light jacket / hoodie for evenings
👟 Comfortable walking shoes
🩴 Flip-flops (for hotel spa & pool)
👙 Swimming suit
☂️ Light rain jacket (just in case)
🕶️ Sunglasses & sunscreen
💻 Laptop or tablet (for group work)
🔌 Phone, charger & power adapter
♻️ Reusable bottle & cup (green project!)
💳 Some cash (EUR)
💊 Personal medication (if any)
📄 ID card or passport & EHIC card
🍝 Snacks/food for Cultural Night

💡 Useful travel tips

  • Taxis in Bansko are affordable — hail one at the bus station or call from reception. Always keep receipts for reimbursement.
  • Check bus schedules and routes at obilet.com or use Google Maps.
  • Most shops and restaurants accept card payments, but keep some cash for smaller places.
  • Tap water is safe and delicious — it flows directly from the Pirin Mountain springs. Refill your reusable bottle instead of buying plastic!
  • Bulgaria uses Type C & F power plugs (230V) — bring an adapter if yours differ.
🇧🇬 Bulgaria Basics

Greetings

Здравей! (Zdravey!)Hello
Добър ден (Dobar den)Good afternoon
Довиждане (Dovizhdane)Goodbye

Politeness

Моля (Molya)Please
Благодаря (Blagodarya)Thank you
Извинете (Izvinete)Excuse me

Getting Around

Къде е...? (Kade e...?)Where is...?
Колко струва? (Kolko struva?)How much is it?
Говорите ли английски? (Govorite li angliyski?)Do you speak English?

Useful Phrases

Да / Не (Da / Ne)Yes / No
Наздраве! (Nazdrave!)Cheers!
⚠️ Fun fact: In Bulgaria, nodding means “no” and shaking your head means “yes” — the opposite of most countries!

Basic info for Bulgaria

Local Currency
Euro (EUR) 💶
Language
Bulgarian
Alphabet
Cyrillic
Time Zone
EET (GMT+2)
Emergency Number
112
Power Plug
Type C / F (230V)
